title: Eabhal (2025) [2nd] 08 - Nicky Kirk
description: 4th October 2025 at the Green Note, London NW1. Folk and Roots, folkandroots.co.uk/. Country: Britain. Style: Traditional Scottish Folk. Lineup: Kaitlin Ross (v). Ewan Duncan (highland bagpipes/flute/whistle). Robbie Greig (fiddle), Megan MacDonald (piano accordion), Nicky Kirk (g), This was the fourth time I have taken photos of Eabhal. The others were in 2016 (www.flickr.com/photos/kmlivemusic/24388540413/), 2019 (www.flickr.com/photos/kmlivemusic/albums/72157711489561642/) and earlier in 2025 (www.flickr.com/photos/kmlivemusic/albums/72177720326028634/). I have also photographed Greig, MacDonald and Ross with other groups (to view click on the musician's name in my Tags). In this photo: Kirk plays an Atkin Guitar. More information: www.eabhal.com/, www.facebook.com/eabhalmusic.

title: UK - London - Lewis Cubitt Square - Santacon 2025_DSC0634
description: My Website : Twitter : Facebook : Instagram : Photocrowd Well, that's a wrap on Christmas 2025...... I was in London a few weeks ago and I happened upon several hundred slightly drunk but good natured Santa's (and Christmas trees) wandering around the Kings Cross area. Turns out it's an annual event called Santacon and anyone can join in. Maybe see you there next year..... Click here for more Christmas related photos : www.flickr.com/photos/darrellg/albums/72157639060428214 From their www.santacon.supermingo.com/ website, " Santacon is a non-profit, non-political, non-religious and non-sensical Christmas parade that traditionally takes place in London one Saturday every December. There's no particular reason to dress up in Santa suits, run around, give gifts, sing songs, have strangers sit on our laps and decide who is naughty or nice. But it's a lot of fun so Santa does it anyway! What you need : A Santa Outfit (not just a hat!), Watch the Facebook page in order to find out when and where, Something Warm (and possibly waterproof), Carol Sheets (download here), Presents for the kids." © D.Godliman
tags: uklondonlewiscubittsquaresantacon2025dsc0634 santacon christmas xmas lewiscubittsquare lewiscubittpark kingscross london unitedkingdom greatbritain britain england gb uk santaclaus santa santas saxophone music saxophonist musician sax streetphotography streetphotographer fun
comments: Another shot: | Some very skilled Santas around, all that racing around delivering pressies, and playing the sax! | [https://www.flickr.com/photos/138552795@N06] I know, and you'd think three weeks before Christmas would be their second busiest time of year. | [https://www.flickr.com/photos/darrellg] I think those elves deserve a pay rise....

title: Big Bus Company no DA209, Reg PF08USB , passing St. Pancras Station on a Big Bus open top Tour. 24.03.2024
John Mitchell & Friends live at Dingwalls, London 18th December 2025
description: John Mitchell and Friends at Dingwall's last night was a great little gig in spite of the band only having rehearsed for 20 minutes on Wednesday apparently! A good selection from It Bites, Lonely Robot and Frost material keeping everyone happy and a star back line of Oskar Holdorff (Big Big Train), Craig Blundell (Frost, Steve Hackett, Steven Wilson) and in-demand session bassist Jez Fielder to help out with vocal support on a few numbers from Leddra Chapman, whose trio also opened the proceedings.

title: Hotel
description: I thought it might be interesting to take a shot of the Premier Inn Cafe/Bar from directly behind the salt and pepper grinders.  In the shot taken in portrait mode the shape of grinders bodies seem to become a subject in themselves and the bright lighting produced quite strong reflections and shadows. The black and white version was chosen as I thought it more strongly emphasised the shapes of the foreground subjects.

description:
Autumn at St Pancras Lock, London, UK
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

title: Also Eden - The Dome Upstairs, London 2nd November 2025
description: Welsh Progsters Also Eden - led by Winter's End guru Huw Lloyd Jones - put in a really good performance opening for their German friends RPWL at the Dome in Tufnell Park. Together with Stephen Lambe (who was on the Merch desk), Huw had managed RPWL's London visit and arranged to share some of Also Eden's gear with them to reduce the post-Brexit complexities of international touring. This was the third time I'd seen Also Eden and their best performance yet.

title: GWL Garden gate
description: Photo originally posted to the Guess Where London Group www.flickr.com/groups/guesswherelondon/
tags: architecture london gate brick guess gwl where
comments: Hither Green Lewisham? | [https://www.flickr.com/photos/balage66] No not in Hither Green | Are we jn LB Barnet then? | [https://www.flickr.com/photos/myupload1] No not LB Barnet either but that is warmer than Lewisham | Apologies all this picture was taken in 2008 and just checking the location I find that not only have I posted here before but it is actually no longer there and has been replaced with a steel gate with an image of this circular brickwork. Well that should give a clue as to where it was. | At least you've saved me trawling though the Open House website :-) | What the same location looks like today. No one has guessed it yet | I'm sure I've seen this pic before, but not in GWL's archives... | Well, I have tracked it down: a house designed and built for himself by the B of ABK, the architects of the original monstrous carbuncle. The whole thing was designed around preserving the london plane tree: unfortunately the tree grew and was knocking the wall over, along with causing the gate to stick. The Burton House, 1B Lady Margaret Road NW5 | [https://www.flickr.com/photos/simon_p_white] Yes you have is thanks for the History
